name: enterpriseai.tools updates dataset
description: High-impact market intelligence for enterprise AI tooling — releases, deprecations, acquisitions, license and certification changes — each with a primary source URL.

updates.summary: AWS added interactive PTY-backed shells to Bedrock AgentCore Runtime, letting teams reconnect into live agent sessions for debugging, file inspection, and terminal-native coding-agent workflows.
updates.sourceTitle: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime introduces interactive shells for terminal access into agent sessions

updates.summary: Microsoft announced Foundry Managed Compute, adding a managed GPU platform for hosting open-source and custom models through the same Foundry endpoint, SDKs, and billing surface used for frontier-model deployments.
updates.sourceTitle: Announcing Foundry Managed Compute: Run open models in Microsoft Foundry

updates.summary: Microsoft Agent Framework 1.7.0 added harness agents, A2A session support, and new Foundry integration hooks, extending the framework's orchestration and inter-agent workflow surface.
AWS added Anthropic Claude Opus 4.8 to Amazon Bedrock, giving Bedrock customers access to Anthropic's latest high-end model inside AWS-managed security and regional controls.
updates.sourceTitle: Claude Opus 4.8 is now available on AWS

updates.summary: Google deprecated Vertex AI Extensions and said the service will shut down after 2026-11-26, directing teams to migrate agent integrations to Agent Platform to avoid disruption.
AWS introduced AgentCore payments in preview, adding managed agent micropayments for APIs, web content, MCP servers, and other agents via x402-backed transaction flows.
updates.sourceTitle: Agents that transact: Introducing Amazon Bedrock AgentCore payments, built with Coinbase and Stripe

updates.summary: Microsoft Agent Framework 1.4.0 introduced experimental skills API breaking changes, migrated its A2A layer to a2a-sdk v1.0, and tightened default DevUI access controls and CORS posture.
LangFlow 1.9.3 shipped as a critical security release with SSRF protection, DNS rebinding prevention, dependency CVE fixes, and an explicit immediate-upgrade recommendation.
Google announced Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite general availability on Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform, adding a lower-latency, lower-cost production model option for agent and orchestration workloads.
updates.sourceTitle: Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite is now generally available

updates.summary: Dify 1.14.1 hardened self-hosted SECRET_KEY handling, protected internal metrics endpoints, fixed tenant-isolation issues, and refreshed dependencies for CVE-2026-42208 and related risks.
updates.sourceTitle: Security hardening, workflow stability, and cleaner self-hosted deployments · langgenius/dify · GitHub

updates.summary: LangGraph 1.2.0 promoted its alpha packages to official releases and added durable error-handler resume across host crashes, improving reliability for stateful production agent workflows.
Foundry Local 1.1 added live transcription, embeddings, and Responses API support, expanding Microsoft’s local/no-cloud deployment path for speech, RAG, and tool-calling workloads.
updates.sourceTitle: Foundry Local 1.1: Live Transcription, Embeddings, and Responses API

updates.summary: Microsoft Agent Framework Python 1.3.0 added class-based skill definitions plus experimental session-mode and todo-list harness context providers, expanding structured agent composition patterns.
OpenAI Agents SDK 0.16.0 changed the default model to gpt-5.4-mini for runs without an explicit model, altering baseline behavior, reasoning defaults, and potential cost/perf expectations.
Mastra 1.32.0 introduced fine-grained authorization checks across agent runs, tool and workflow execution, memory thread access, server adapters, and MCP paths.
Google made 50+ fully managed MCP servers available for Google Cloud services, expanding protocol-aligned tool integration options for Vertex AI and Gemini Enterprise agent builds.
updates.sourceTitle: 50+ fully managed MCP servers now available for Google Cloud services

updates.summary: AWS announced Amazon Bedrock AgentCore availability in the South America (São Paulo) Region, extending managed agent runtime coverage into an additional geography.
updates.sourceTitle: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore is now available in the South America (São Paulo) Region

updates.summary: LangFlow 1.9.2 added telemetry to deployment APIs and shipped fixes across env var resolution, Google embeddings dimensions, and related deployment/runtime paths.
OpenAI Agents SDK 0.15.0 changed refusal handling to raise ModelRefusalError explicitly instead of surfacing empty outputs, a behavior change that can affect run-loop integrations.
CrewAI 1.14.4 added Azure OpenAI Responses API support, Azure AI Inference credential-scope forwarding, Vertex AI workload identity guidance, and new research/search tools integrations.
Dify 1.14.0 introduced collaborative workflow editing with synced graph updates and presence indicators, while keeping collaboration disabled by default for self-hosted deployments.
Microsoft Agent Framework Python 1.2.2 added an Azure AI Content Understanding context provider for documents, images, audio, and video, expanding enterprise attachment analysis paths.
Paperclip v2026.428.0 added pause/resume controls for agents in the sidebar and improved handling for long issue threads, extending day-2 control-plane usability.
Semantic Kernel Python 1.41.3 added SQL Server field and table name escaping and extended in-memory collection filter blocklists, tightening connector safety for production use.
LangFlow 1.9.1 shipped security fixes for MCP path traversal, cross-user disclosure, upload auth, IDOR, XSS handling, and webhook auth defaults, alongside Gemini 3 tool-calling updates.
CrewAI 1.14.3 added Bedrock V4 support, checkpoint and fork support for standalone agents, Azure DefaultAzureCredential fallback, and security dependency patches.

Microsoft Agent Framework Python 1.2.0 added a functional workflow API, OpenTelemetry support for GitHubCopilotAgent, and an A2A bridge, extending interoperability and observability for production agent deployments.
Microsoft announced hosted agents preview, Toolbox preview, memory preview, and Foundry Toolkit for VS Code GA as part of the developer path from local builds to production deployment.
updates.sourceTitle: From Local to Production: The Complete Developer Journey for Building, Composing, and Deploying AI Agents

updates.summary: Google launched Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform, folding Vertex AI and Agent Builder capabilities under a renamed platform with Agent Runtime, Agent Registry, Agent Identity, and related governance surfaces.
updates.sourceTitle: Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform release notes

updates.summary: Haystack 2.28.0 introduced breaking API changes around request exception handling, LLM initialization, and agent invocation signatures, with new runtime State injection for tools.
Flowise's April 14 release focused on security hardening, including a fix for a hardcoded CORS wildcard on the TTS endpoint and additional MCP server configuration protections.
updates.title: Flowise release tightens security around CORS and MCP config

updates.summary: CrewAI posted a new pre-release on April 14 with resume hints for devtools failure handling and a Bedrock Converse API strict-mode fix, extending its agent runtime integrations.
updates.title: CrewAI pre-release adds Bedrock strict-mode fixes

updates.summary: n8n published pre-release 2.17.0 on April 13 with AI-builder credential-context changes plus fixes across PostHog, NASA, Peekalink, Clearbit, and Uptime Robot integrations.
updates.title: n8n 2.17.0 pre-release expands AI-builder integration fixes

updates.summary: The OpenAI Agents SDK April 9 release hardened SQLite and SQLAlchemy session handling and expanded tracing documentation, signaling continued stabilization of the production agent stack.
updates.title: OpenAI Agents SDK release hardens tracing and SQL session handling

updates.summary: Semantic Kernel's April 8 release included prompt-template serialization improvements and dependency updates, keeping Microsoft's orchestration SDK active as Agent Framework work continues.
updates.title: Semantic Kernel refreshes prompt serialization and dependencies

updates.summary: Langflow's April 7 release added Windows Playwright coverage to nightly builds and refreshed desktop documentation, indicating continued maintenance after the DataStax-to-IBM deal.
updates.title: Langflow release adds Windows Playwright coverage

updates.summary: Haystack's April 1 release highlighted automatic list joining inside pipelines, simplifying multi-input workflow construction without extra glue components.
updates.title: Haystack simplifies multi-input pipelines with automatic list joining

updates.summary: Dify v1.13.3 shipped on March 27 with workflow, streaming, and knowledge-retrieval stability fixes, reinforcing its push toward more reliable production orchestration.
updates.title: Dify 1.13.3 ships workflow and retrieval stability fixes

updates.summary: GitHub announced that, from April 24, interaction data from Copilot Free, Pro, and Pro+ users will be used for model improvement unless users opt out, making data-governance review more important for enterprises.
updates.sourceTitle: Updates to GitHub Copilot interaction data usage policy
updates.title: GitHub Copilot expands model-training use of interaction data

updates.summary: LlamaIndex's March 16 release notes show coordinated package updates across the agent stack, including AgentMesh and Azure-related modules, alongside deprecation of Python 3.9 support.
updates.title: LlamaIndex coordinates agent-stack package updates and drops Python 3.9

updates.summary: NeMo Guardrails' March 12 release introduced IORails for parallel input/output safety execution, plus OpenAI-compatible server support and standalone async rail validation.
updates.title: NeMo Guardrails adds parallel IORails safety execution

updates.summary: Check Point announced an agreement to acquire Lakera, positioning the startup's AI-native protection stack inside a broader end-to-end enterprise AI security portfolio.
updates.sourceTitle: Check Point Acquires Lakera to Deliver End-to-End AI Security for Enterprises
updates.title: Check Point moves to acquire Lakera

updates.summary: IBM said it would acquire DataStax, explicitly naming Langflow as part of the transaction and tying the low-code AI builder into IBM's watsonx roadmap.
updates.sourceTitle: IBM to Acquire DataStax, Deepening watsonx Capabilities and Addressing Generative AI Data Needs for the Enterprise
updates.title: IBM announces DataStax acquisition, bringing Langflow into scope
